name: ChurnZero Customer Success API
what_it_does: ChurnZero REST API for customer success platform focused on churn prevention and product adoption. Enables AI agents to query customer health scores, track feature usage, access journey data, manage plays/tasks, and integrate customer intelligence into workflows. Used by SaaS CSM teams to drive retention.
use_cases: Querying customer health scores and churn risk signals from CS workflow agents
Retrieving product adoption and feature usage data from retention analytics agents
Managing customer journeys and success milestones from CSM automation agents
Triggering plays and tasks based on health score thresholds from retention agents
not_for: Teams without SaaS subscription business models
Consumer apps (ChurnZero is B2B SaaS focused)
best_when: A SaaS company using ChurnZero wants AI agents to surface churn risk, trigger CS plays, and integrate customer health intelligence into support and sales workflows.
avoid_when: PRIVACY: Customer usage data and health scores are sensitive business intelligence. Autonomous CS actions (cancellations, interventions) require human CSM oversight.

auth.notes: ChurnZero API key authentication. Keys available in ChurnZero admin settings. Account-scoped access.

pricing.notes: Enterprise CS platform for mid-market and enterprise SaaS. API access included with subscription.

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: Customer health scores and usage data are sensitive business intelligence
No public MCP server — REST API integration required
API documentation quality varies — contact ChurnZero support for full API access
Customer success interventions require human CSM oversight — agents should surface, not act
Rate limits undocumented — throttle requests for large account portfolios
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: Customer health scores, usage patterns, and SaaS adoption data. SOC2 and GDPR. API key auth. CS interventions need human oversight.

security_score_components.security_notes: Customer usage data and health scoring is proprietary business intelligence. SOC2 and GDPR.
